introSection: Figure Eight was a wooden side friction roller coaster located on the seafront in Coogee, New South Wales, Australia. It was opened in 1907. It was demolished in 1912 and was reportedly replaced by a hotel and an extension to the adjacent baths. The "Coogee Palace" this roller coaster stood beside was rebuilt in 1987 and now operates as the "Coogee Pavillion".
fullText: Figure Eight was a wooden side friction roller coaster located on the seafront in Coogee, New South Wales, Australia. It was opened in 1907. It was demolished in 1912 and was reportedly replaced by a hotel and an extension to the adjacent baths. The "Coogee Palace" this roller coaster stood beside was rebuilt in 1987 and now operates as the "Coogee Pavillion".
